description_long: This is a solid-body electric guitar with a single cutaway design, reminiscent of the classic Fender Telecaster style. The body is finished in a light beige or butterscotch color, with a contrasting black pickguard covering the area below the strings and pickups. The neck is a lighter wood tone, likely maple, and features a bolt-on construction with visible frets and dot inlays. The bridge and control plate are metallic, and the guitar is equipped with two single-coil pickups, one near the bridge and one in the neck position. The headstock is not fully visible, but the overall silhouette and hardware placement suggest a traditional six-string electric guitar. This instrument is commonly seen in urban environments such as music venues, street performances, and studios.
description: A beige solid-body electric guitar with a black pickguard, two single-coil pickups, and a light wood neck, styled after a classic Telecaster.
description_view_0: Front view showing the beige body, black pickguard, two single-coil pickups, control plate, and full neck with visible frets.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the guitar's thin body depth and the layered construction of the body and neck.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, unadorned back of the beige body and the back of the neck.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the body thickness and the edge of the pickguard.
